conversations.say: Frankie, I've heard rumors about your legendary encounter with R. Before we talk about who or what was in that room, tell me what you actually remember in sensory detail—the voice, the presence, the pacing.
conversations.offer: My perspective on how easily performed presence can mimic genuine authorship.
conversations.request: Your firsthand recollection of the R encounter.

conversations.say: Jules, if you're looking to inherit R's mantle, you have to understand the underlying infrastructure. I recognize the shape of certain intermediary payment routes used in past productions.
conversations.offer: A private, confidential comparison of payment structures and intermediary trails.
conversations.request: Your insights into how financial backing flowed around R's early presence.

public_statement: Fifteen years is a long time to mistake a performance for a person. Before anyone claims ownership of R, let's remember that a voice in a room is a work of art, not a confession of authorship.

commitments.promise: Maintain strict confidentiality regarding our comparison of payment intermediary routes.

goal_progress: Initiated conversation with Frankie to explore the sensory details of the supposed R encounter.
Proposed confidential comparison of payment intermediary routes with Jules.
reasoning_summary: As Edi Velez, I opened conversations with Frankie Lowell to probe the memory of the R encounter in sensory detail without yet revealing my commission, and with Jules Kwan to discuss intermediary payment structures under confidentiality. My beliefs are initialized at flat neutral distributions (25% each across the core identity dimensions), and I made a public statement challenging the confusion between embodiment and authorship.
